specialties.name: Postal Clerk
specialties.what_it_is: Postal Clerks run the Marine Corps' military post offices. They accept, sort, manifest and dispatch personal and official mail, sell stamps and USPS money orders, handle registered mail that can contain classified material, and set up mail service for deployed units. It is a customer-service and logistics job inside a post office, on base and in the field.

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: The Postal Operations Course is DoD military-postal training, not USPS employment training. It does not make you a USPS employee and gives no hiring preference beyond standard veterans' preference — USPS still requires you to apply, pass its entry assessment and be hired competitively. COOL's high-attainability credentials for 0161 are general office certs (Microsoft Office Specialist, Certified Logistics Associate), not postal credentials.

specialties.name: Counterintelligence/Human Intelligence (CI/HUMINT) Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: CI/HUMINT Specialists collect intelligence from people rather than from sensors. They run source operations, conduct interrogations and debriefings, screen local nationals, and do counterintelligence work to detect and disrupt foreign intelligence collection against Marine units. The job is heavy on interviewing, report writing and operating in foreign environments.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: Minimum GT score of 105, or a minimum CL score of 105, or a minimum AFQT score of 55
entry_requirements.afqt_note: NAVMC 1200.1L also accepts a minimum AFQT score of 55 as an alternative to the GT/CL composite.
entry_requirements.security_clearance: Must meet sensitive compartmented information (SCI) eligibility guidelines based on a single scope background investigation/T5 investigation. (12) Must be a volunteer corporal, sergeant, or staff sergeant of any MOS. Lance Corporals and gunnery sergeants may apply for lateral move if determined exceptionally qualified by the 0211 MOS specialist, 02XX occupational field manager, and/or MOS sponsor. Lance Corporals will not receive PMOS 0211 until successful completion of the PMOS 0211 producing school and achieving the rank of corporal. (13) Waivers to any prerequisites or requirements must be submitted to and approved by the 02XX occupational field manager
entry_requirements.other: NOT AN ENTRY-LEVEL MOS. Per NAVMC 1200.1L this MOS is awarded by lateral move or promotion from another PMOS — a civilian cannot enlist directly into it.

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: This is a lateral-move MOS: you cannot enlist directly into 0211. You must already be a Marine, be 21 by graduation, pass a suitability assessment run by CI/HUMINT personnel, and hold SCI eligibility. The training builds interrogation and source-handling skill but confers no federal law-enforcement credential — FBI, DIA and police-detective roles all run their own selection and academy. Realistically the TS/SCI clearance you carry out is worth more on the civilian market than the MOS code.

specialties.name: Intelligence Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Intelligence Analysts fuse reporting from every intelligence discipline — human, signals, imagery, geospatial — into all-source assessments for a commander. They maintain the enemy picture, produce briefs and estimates, and support targeting and planning.

entry_requirements.security_clearance: Must have a current single scope background investigation/T5 investigation and adjudicated for sensitive compartmented information (SCI) eligibility
entry_requirements.other: NOT AN ENTRY-LEVEL MOS. Per NAVMC 1200.1L this MOS is awarded by lateral move or promotion from another PMOS — a civilian cannot enlist directly into it.

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: 0239 is a necessary MOS (NMOS), not an entry MOS: you must already hold PMOS 0211, 0231, 0241, 0261 or 2629. NAVMC 1200.1L lists 'Related Standard Occupational Classification (SOC): None' for it — the Marine Corps itself publishes no civilian equivalent — and Marine Corps COOL has no page for it, so no credential list exists. Analytic tradecraft does transfer to federal and contractor intelligence work, but that path is restricted on the TS/SCI clearance, not on the MOS.

specialties.name: Imagery Analysis Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Imagery Intelligence Specialists exploit pictures and sensor data — satellite and aircraft imagery, infrared, radar, electro-optical — to work out what an adversary has and is doing. They measure and annotate imagery, build imagery-intelligence products, and brief what they found.

specialties.name: Target Mensuration Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Target Mensuration Analysts do the precise measurement work behind targeting: deriving exact coordinates and dimensions from imagery to the accuracy standard required before a weapon can be employed, and running collateral-damage estimation. CJCSI 3505.01 requires this work be done by certified personnel.

They collect and process geophysical and geographic data, run GIS software, produce maps and terrain analyses, and answer how ground, weather and infrastructure will affect an operation.

specialties.what_it_is: Reconnaissance Marines get eyes on the enemy before anyone else does. They insert by land, sea, air or parachute, move undetected, watch and report, and get out. The job is restricted on water ability — the manual requires advanced water-survival qualification plus a reconnaissance physical assessment and aquatic competency test.

specialties.name: Logistics Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Logistics Specialists plan and coordinate how a unit gets supplied and moved — tracking supply, maintenance and transportation requirements, building embarkation and distribution plans, and keeping the materiel picture straight for the commander.

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: One of the better-transferring MOSs, and COOL maps it to Logisticians (SOC 13-1081) — but COOL funds only two high-attainability credentials for 0441 (a Certified Logistics Associate and a CDL), and many civilian logistician postings ask for a bachelor's degree. The MOS gives you real experience; the degree or the CLA/CDL is what gets you the interview.

specialties.name: Airborne and Air Delivery Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Airborne and Air Delivery Specialists are the Corps' parachute riggers. They pack, inspect and maintain the parachutes and airdrop equipment used to drop people and cargo out of aircraft, and they rig loads for air delivery. They are jump-qualified and jump with the gear they pack.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: CL score of 95 or EL score of 95 or GT score of 100 or higher
entry_requirements.security_clearance: Secret security clearance eligibility
entry_requirements.education: High school diploma (Marine Corps enlistment standard)
entry_requirements.physical: Must have initial recruit training swim qualification of WS-B (+) water survival qualification. army physical fitness test prior to shipping to Airborne Course (A030CG1), U. army physical fitness test upon checking into the U.

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: One of the few MOSs with a genuine civilian credential attached: COOL lists Senior Parachute Rigger as a high-attainability credential. But the FAA Parachute Rigger certificate is a separate FAA rating (14 CFR Part 65) — military rigging experience makes you eligible to sit for it, it is not automatically awarded with the MOS. Chase the FAA rating before you separate, or the skill does not travel.

specialties.name: Personnel Retrieval and Processing Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Personnel Retrieval and Processing Specialists handle the dead. They search for and recover human remains, process and tentatively identify them, handle personal effects, and manage interment, disinterment and transportation — in combat and in disaster response. The manual requires you to volunteer for it.

civilian_crosswalk.salary_source: BLS OES May 2024 (national), SOC 39-4021 — Funeral Attendants: 10th pct $26,820, median $34,610, 90th pct $46,690
civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: You are trained in fatality management, not funeral service. This does NOT make you a licensed funeral director or embalmer: every state licenses those through mortuary-science schooling (typically an associate degree), a national board exam and an apprenticeship. COOL does list the National Board Examination as a high-attainability credential, which is the right target if this is the career you want — but the licence still requires the civilian schooling.

specialties.name: Personnel Retrieval and Processing Technician
specialties.what_it_is: Personnel Retrieval and Processing Technicians are the more senior remains-handling specialists, working in joint and single-service theatres of operations on the recovery, processing and repatriation of human remains.

civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: The same gap as 0471, and sharper: military mortuary affairs is not a path to a funeral-director or embalmer licence, which every state issues on the basis of mortuary-science education, the National Board Exam and an apprenticeship. Marine Corps COOL has no page for 0472, so no credential list is published for it at all. The manual also requires a 7-month combat-theatre deployment under school-trained PRP supervision.

specialties.name: Logistics/Mobility Chief
specialties.what_it_is: The Logistics/Mobility Chief is the senior enlisted planner for logistics, embarkation and landing-support operations — moving units and their equipment by sea, air and land.

specialties.name: MAGTF Planning Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: MAGTF Planning Specialists do force-deployment planning: building and maintaining the data that says which units and equipment move where and when, supporting Global Force Management and Joint Combat Capability Assessment, and keeping the deployment picture accurate for planners.

entry_requirements.asvab_line_score: CL score of 100 or GT score of 100 or higher
entry_requirements.security_clearance: (a) Must be eligible for a top secret (TS) security clearance and access to Sensitive Compartmented Information (SCI) predicated on a completed single scope background investigation (SSBI). Application for TS security clearance must be submitted during recruit training or prior to attendance of the MAGTF Planner Basic Course (N46KAG2), MARDET Dam Neck, Virginia Beach, VA. (b) Marines with the MOS 0511 who are re-enlisting must possess an adjudicated Secret security clearance and are required to submit a application for a top-secret security clearance prior to finalizing their re- enlistment. Interim Secret security clearances shall not be authorized. (c) Marines conducting lateral moves to the 0511 MOS must possess an adjudicated secret security clearance. Upon favorable recommendation from MOS screening interview, lateral move request Marines are required to submit an application for a top-secret security clearance prior to attending the MAGTF Planner Basic Course. Students arriving with interim secret clearances will not be allowed to complete the training

specialties.what_it_is: Civil Affairs Specialists are the link between a Marine unit and the civilian population around it. They conduct civil reconnaissance, engage local leaders and institutions, and plan civil-military operations so the civil environment supports rather than obstructs the mission.
